text.title: Eloquent Repository Pattern
text.summary: Optional repository layer that encapsulates eager loading, pagination, and write operations; adopt only when a swap-out case exists.

rule.statement: Eager-load in repository methods by default; never let controllers or resources trigger lazy associations.
rule.rationale: N+1 bugs caused by lazy loading in controllers are invisible in code review. Forcing eager loading at the repository boundary makes query cost explicit and reviewable.

rule.statement: Wrap multi-write paths in DB::transaction(); Eloquent does not auto-run transactions for create + relation->attach combinations.
rule.rationale: Without an explicit transaction, a failed attach after a successful create leaves an orphaned record with no rollback.

antipattern.description: Repository added for all aggregates "for consistency" but bypassed 50% of the time via Model::query() directly.
antipattern.reason: Partial adoption is the worst outcome: the team gets ceremony without the swap-out benefit, and two patterns coexist. Either commit fully (no Model::query() outside the repo) or skip the repository entirely.
